title: Test statistic for slope
description: Calculate the test statistic in a t test for slope.
topic_path: Testing for the slope of a regression model

assessment_items.question_content:
Jian obtained a random sample of data on how long it took each of $24$ students to complete a timed reaction game and a timed memory game. He noticed a positive linear relationship between the times on each task. Here is computer output on the sample data:

|| Summary statistics ||
Variable | n | Mean | StDev | SE Mean
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
$x=\text{reaction time}$ | $24$ | $0.398$ | $0.133$ | $0.027$
$y=\text{memory time}$ | $24$ | $43.042$ | $8.554$ | $1.746$

|| Regression: memory vs. reaction ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $37.200$ | $5.579$
**Reaction** | $14.686$ | $13.329$
$\text{S}=8.515$| $\text{R-sq}=5.2\%$ |

Assume that all conditions for inference have been met.

**Which of these is an appropriate test statistic for testing the null hypothesis that the population slope in this setting is $0$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The formula for the test statistic

The test statistic gives us an idea of how far away our sample result is from our null hypothesis. For a $t$ test about slope, our test statistic is:

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\blueD{\text{statistic}}-\greenD{\text{parameter}}}{\goldD{\text{standard error of statistic}}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{\blueD{b} -\greenD{\beta_0}}{\goldD{\text{SE}_b}}
\end{align}$

$\blueD{b}$ is the sample slope of regression line.

$\greenD{\beta_0}$ is the hypothesized slope (which is $0$ in this case).

$\goldD{\text{SE}_b}$ is the standard error of the sample slope.

###Components of formula

|| Regression: memory vs. reaction ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
Constant | $37.200$ | $5.579$
Reaction | $\blueD{14.686}$ | $\goldD{13.329}$
$\text{S}=8.515$| $\text{R-sq}=5.2\%$ |

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\blueD{b} -\greenD{\beta_0}}{\goldD{\text{SE}_b}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{\blueD{14.686} -\greenD{0}}{\goldD{13.329}}
\end{align}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic is

$t=\dfrac{14.686}{13.329}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Mekhi is studying the trend of the world's average temperature over time. He collects data about the world's average temperature between the years $1970$ and $2011$ (a total of $42$ years). Here is computer output on the sample data (years are counted as number of years since $1970$):

|| Summary statistics ||
Variable | n | Mean | StDev | SE Mean
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
$x=\text{ year}$ | $42$ | $20.5$ | $12.27$ | $1.89$
$y=\text{ temperature}$ | $42$ | $14.31$ | $0.23$ | $0.04$

|| Regression: temperature vs. year ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $13.964$ | $0.028$
**Year** | $0.0167$ | $0.001$
$\text{S}=0.092$| $\text{R-sq}=83.83\%$ |

Assume that all conditions for inference have been met.

**Which of these is an appropriate test statistic for testing the null hypothesis that the population slope in this setting is $0$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The formula for the test statistic

The test statistic gives us an idea of how far away our sample result is from our null hypothesis. For a $t$ test about slope, our test statistic is:

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\blueD{\text{statistic}}-\greenD{\text{parameter}}}{\goldD{\text{standard error of statistic}}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{\blueD{b} -\greenD{\beta_0}}{\goldD{\text{SE}_b}}
\end{align}$

$\blueD{b}$ is the sample slope of regression line.

$\greenD{\beta_0}$ is the hypothesized slope (which is $0$ in this case).

$\goldD{\text{SE}_b}$ is the standard error of the sample slope.

###Components of formula

|| Regression: temperature vs. year ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $13.964$ | $0.028$
**Year** | $\blueD{0.0167}$ | $\goldD{0.001}$
$\text{S}=0.092$| $\text{R-sq}=83.83\%$ |

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\blueD{b} -\greenD{\beta_0}}{\goldD{\text{SE}_b}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{\blueD{0.0167} -\greenD{0}}{\goldD{0.001}}
\end{align}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic is

$t=\dfrac{0.0167}{0.001}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Elvin collected the scores of a random sample $41$ students on the first exam in a certain class and their corresponding scores on the second exam in that class. Here is computer output on the sample data:

|| Summary statistics ||
Variable | n | Mean | StDev | SE Mean
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
$x=\text{ first exam score}$ | $41$ | $59.5$ | $19.7$ | $3.1$
$y=\text{ second exam score}$ | $41$ | $59.4$ | $21.7$ | $3.4$

|| Regression: second exam score vs. first exam score ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $6.985$ | $6.65$
**First exam score** | $0.881$ | $0.11$
$\text{S}=13.2$ | $\text{R-sq}=62.89\%$ |

Assume that all conditions for inference have been met.

**Which of these is an appropriate test statistic for testing the null hypothesis that the population slope in this setting is $0$?**

hints:

\end{align}$

$\blueD{b}$ is the sample slope of regression line.

$\greenD{\beta_0}$ is the hypothesized slope (which is $0$ in this case).

$\goldD{\text{SE}_b}$ is the standard error of the sample slope.

###Components of formula

|| Regression: second exam score vs. first exam score ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $6.985$ | $6.65$
**First exam score** | $\blueD{0.881}$ | $\goldD{0.11}$
$\text{S}=13.2$ | $\text{R-sq}=62.89\%$ |

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\blueD{b} -\greenD{\beta_0}}{\goldD{\text{SE}_b}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{\blueD{0.881} -\greenD{0}}{\goldD{0.11}}
\end{align}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic is

$t=\dfrac{0.881}{0.11}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Christine collected data about the weight (in thousand $\text{kg}$) and fuel efficiency (in $\text{km}$ per $\text{L}$) of a random sample of $32$ car models. Here is computer output on the sample data:

|| Summary statistics ||
Variable | n | Mean | StDev | SE Mean
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
$x=\text{ weight}$ | $32$ | $3.2$ | $0.98$ | $0.17$
$y=\text{ efficiency}$ | $32$ | $20.1$ | $6.03$ | $1.07$

|| Regression: efficiency vs. weight ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $15.84$ | $0.8$
**Weight** | $-5$ | $0.52$
$\text{S}=1.294$| $\text{R-sq}=75.28\%$ |

Assume that all conditions for inference have been met.

**Which of these is an appropriate test statistic for testing the null hypothesis that the population slope in this setting is $0$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The formula for the test statistic

The test statistic gives us an idea of how far away our sample result is from our null hypothesis. For a $t$ test about slope, our test statistic is:

\end{align}$

$\blueD{b}$ is the sample slope of regression line.

$\greenD{\beta_0}$ is the hypothesized slope (which is $0$ in this case).

$\goldD{\text{SE}_b}$ is the standard error of the sample slope.

###Components of formula

|| Regression: efficiency vs. weight ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $15.84$ | $0.8$
**Weight** | $\blueD{-5}$ | $\goldD{0.52}$
$\text{S}=1.294$| $\text{R-sq}=75.28\%$ |

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\blueD{b} -\greenD{\beta_0}}{\goldD{\text{SE}_b}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{\blueD{-5} -\greenD{0}}{\goldD{0.52}}
\end{align}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic is

$t=\dfrac{-5}{0.52}$

assessment_items.question_content:

assessment_items.question_content:

assessment_items.question_content:

assessment_items.question_content:
Ona took a random sample of $20$ soccer teams across Europe, and tracked the average number of goals each team scored per match, and how many total matches each team won, in the $2014\,\text{-}\,2015$ season. Here is regression output on the sample data:

|| Regression: wins vs. goals  ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $-3.64 $ | $1.54$
**Goals** | $14.02$ | $1.15$
$\text{S}=1.951$| $\text{R-sq}=88.56\%$ |

Assume that all conditions for inference have been met.

**Which of these is an appropriate test statistic for testing the null hypothesis that the population slope in this setting is $0$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:

\end{align}$

$\greenD{\beta_0}$ is the hypothesized slope (which is $0$ in this case).

$\goldD{\text{SE}_b}$ is the standard error of the sample slope.

###Components of formula

|| Regression: wins vs. goals  ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $-3.64 $ | $1.54$
**Goals** | $\blueD{14.02}$ | $\goldD{1.15}$
$\text{S}=1.951$| $\text{R-sq}=88.56\%$ |

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\blueD{b} -\greenD{\beta_0}}{\goldD{\text{SE}_b}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{\blueD{14.02} -\greenD{0}}{\goldD{1.15}}
\end{align}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic is

$t=\dfrac{14.02}{1.15}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Cerro Negro is an active volcano located in Nicaragua. Amina gathered data on the date and volume (in thousand cubic meters) of Cerro Negro's $23$ recent eruptions. Here is regression output on the sample data (years are counted as number of years since 1850):

|| Regression: volume vs. year ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $-3984$ | $13{,}390$
**Year** | $1198$ | $131$
$\text{S}=22{,}744$| $\text{R-sq}=79.84\%$ |

Assume that all conditions for inference have been met.

**Which of these is an appropriate test statistic for testing the null hypothesis that the population slope in this setting is $0$?**

$\goldD{\text{SE}_b}$ is the standard error of the sample slope.

###Components of formula

Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
**Constant** | $-3984$ | $13390$
**Year** | $\blueD{1198}$ | $\goldD{131}$
$\text{S}=22744$| $\text{R-sq}=79.84\%$ |

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\blueD{b} -\greenD{\beta_0}}{\goldD{\text{SE}_b}} \\\\

\end{align}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic is

assessment_items.question_content:
Kadence took a random sample of $24$ students at her school and asked them how much they typically slept on a school night versus a weekend night. Here is computer output from a least-squares regression analysis on her sample:

|| Regression: weekend vs. school night ||
Predictor | Coef | SE Coef
